era_context: Late Archaic Greece; Lydian Empire under Croesus; Neo-Babylonian Empire under Nabonidus; Eastern Zhou period in China (Spring and Autumn period); early Iron Age Mediterranean and Near East.

events.title: Possible conflict or diplomatic activity in China (Spring and Autumn period)
events.region: China (Eastern Zhou, Spring and Autumn period)
events.description: The Spring and Autumn Annals (Chunqiu) record events year by year, though many entries are brief and symbolic. The year 578 BCE falls within the period of interstate conflict in China, but surviving records do not preserve specific details for this year. The broader context involves ongoing warfare between states like Jin, Chu, Qi, and others, with shifting alliances and territorial disputes.
events.certainty_note: The Chunqiu provides year-by-year records, but specific events for 578 BCE are not distinctly preserved or memorable in surviving sources. The broader context of interstate conflict is certain; specific events for this year are uncertain.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified. The sparse record for this year reflects the limitations of historical documentation rather than any disputed claims.
historiographic_note: The year 578 BCE is poorly documented in surviving sources. Greek sources provide no specific events. Mesopotamian sources (cuneiform) do not record specific events for this year. Chinese sources (Chunqiu) may contain entries, but they are not distinctive or well-preserved in later compilations. The historical record for this year is minimal.
